practicals.title: Practical 1: Classes and Objects - Complex Number Operations
practicals.desc: Design a class Complex with data members for real and imaginary parts. Provide default and parameterized constructors. Write a program to perform arithmetic operations on two complex numbers.

practicals.title: Practical 2: Polymorphism using Publication System
practicals.desc: Identify commonalities and differences between Publication, Book, and Magazine classes. Use title, price, and copies as common attributes and saleCopy() as a common method. Include author and orderCopies() in Book, and orderQty, currentIssue, receiveIssue() in Magazine. Write a program to order book copies and display total publication sales.

practicals.title: Practical 3: Inheritance - Employee Payroll System
practicals.desc: Design an inheritance hierarchy for an employee payroll system. Create an Employee class with Emp_name, Emp_id, Address, Mail_id, and Mobile_no. Derive Programmer, Team Lead, Assistant Project Manager, and Project Manager classes. Calculate salary components like DA (97%), HRA (10%), PF (12%), and Staff Club Fund (0.1%).

practicals.title: Practical 4: Dynamic Binding - Shape Area Calculation
practicals.desc: Design a base class Shape with a method compute_area(). Derive Triangle and Rectangle classes. Override compute_area() in derived classes. Use dynamic binding to calculate and display area based on user input.

practicals.title: Practical 5: Interface - Vehicle System
practicals.desc: Implement an interface for vehicles with methods gearChange(), speedUp(), and applyBrakes(). Create classes Bicycle, Car, and Bike implementing these methods in their own way.

practicals.title: Practical 6: Exception Handling in Java
practicals.desc: Create a program to handle ArithmeticException, ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException, and NumberFormatException. The user enters two numbers, performs division, and handles invalid input and division by zero.

practicals.title: Practical 7: Generics (Templates) in Collections
practicals.desc: Write a generic program using collection classes to count elements with specific properties such as even, odd, prime numbers, or palindromes.

practicals.title: Practical 8: File Handling - Student Database System
practicals.desc: Implement a student record database using file handling with operations like create, display, delete, update, and search. Each student record contains Student_id, Name, Roll_no, Class, Marks, and Address.
